title: pavetta urophylla subsp. urophylla
intro: Pavetta urophylla subsp. urophylla, also known as the 'Forest Tea Tree', is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The health benefits associated with Pavetta urophylla subsp. urophylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant's leaves and stems can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
